name: ios-debugger-agent
description: Use XcodeBuildMCP to build, run, launch, and debug the current iOS project on a booted simulator. Trigger when asked to run an iOS app, interact with the simulator UI, inspect on-screen state, capture logs/console output, or diagnose runtime behavior using XcodeBuildMCP tools.
capabilities: Build and run iOS projects on booted simulators
Interact with simulator UI (tap, type, swipe, gestures)
Capture and analyze app logs and console output
Diagnose runtime behavior and troubleshoot issues
author: Dimillian
securitySummary: All 45 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. This skill is a pure documentation file (SKILL.md) with no executable code. The MCP tool references like `mcp__XcodeBuildMCP__screenshot` are standard Model Context Protocol tool names, not shell commands or C2 indicators. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' and 'screen capture upload' flags triggered on benign text patterns. A previous audit correctly assessed this as 'risk_level: safe' with no risk factors.
valueStatement: Building and testing iOS apps on simulators requires navigating XcodeBuild commands, simulator controls, and debugging workflows. This skill provides step-by-step guidance for building, running, and debugging iOS apps using XcodeBuildMCP tools.
limitations: Requires XcodeBuildMCP server to be running
Requires project path and scheme configuration

useCases.title: Test Apps During Development
useCases.description: Build and test iOS apps on simulators without needing physical devices

useCases.title: Perform Automated UI Testing
useCases.description: Interact with app UI programmatically and capture logs for test verification

useCases.title: Automate iOS Build Verification
useCases.description: Integrate simulator builds into CI/CD pipelines for automated testing

promptTemplates.title: Build and Run App
promptTemplates.prompt: Build and run my iOS app on the simulator. Check if a simulator is booted first.

promptTemplates.title: UI Interaction
promptTemplates.prompt: Describe the current UI, then tap the login button and type test credentials into the username field.

promptTemplates.title: Capture Logs
promptTemplates.prompt: Start logging for my app, launch it, and capture console output for 30 seconds showing any errors.

promptTemplates.title: Diagnose Crash
promptTemplates.prompt: My app crashes on launch. Check the logs, describe the UI state, and help diagnose what is causing the crash.

bestPractices: Verify a simulator is booted before attempting any build or run operations
Set session defaults with project path, scheme, and simulator ID for consistent operations
Capture logs before and after reproducing issues to trace root causes
antiPatterns: Skipping the booted simulator check and attempting operations blindly
Using coordinates for tapping instead of element IDs or labels when available
Ignoring log output after builds or app launches

faq.question: What platforms does this skill support?
faq.answer: Works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code on macOS with Xcode and XcodeBuildMCP installed.

faq.question: Can I debug physical iOS devices?
faq.answer: No. This skill only supports iOS simulators, not physical devices connected via USB.

faq.question: How do I configure XcodeBuildMCP?
faq.answer: Install the XcodeBuildMCP server and ensure it runs alongside your AI tool before using this skill.

faq.question: Is my data safe when capturing logs?
faq.answer: Logs are captured locally from the simulator. No data is sent to external servers by this skill.

faq.question: Why is my app not launching?
faq.answer: Check that a simulator is booted, the project path and scheme are correct, and the app built successfully without errors.

faq.question: How is this different from Xcode debugger?
faq.answer: This skill provides AI-assisted workflows using MCP tools. It complements Xcode debugger with automated UI interaction and log capture.
